title: Share your opinions on a research on chart similarity
author: Hot_Style5572
selftext:
I’m working on a research project around a problem that’s always bothered me in technical chart-based trading:

**we talk about “similar setups”, but we never actually define similarity.**

Most pattern strategies rely on visual judgment or hand-crafted indicators, which makes backtesting highly subjective and hard to reproduce.

So I’m building a system that takes two price series and outputs a **similarity score** — a deterministic number representing how similar their price behavior actually is.

Conceptually:

* If two charts have a high similarity score, they should be analyzed the same way. And if the setup carries an edge, the outcomes will display a tendency to one direction over the other.
* If they don’t, applying the same strategy is probably invalid.

The system:

* Works directly on raw OHLC time series (no handcrafted indicators) for now - planning to add more ingredients as we go: volume, volatility etc.
* Allows searching historical data for similar patterns
* Returns distributions of outcomes for those conditions

The goal is to make chart-based research:

* reproducible
* statistically testable
* and less dependent on human bias

It’s still in early testing, and I’m looking for technically-minded traders / ML people who:

* do systematic backtesting
* build or use AI trading systems
* or care about quantitative validation of discretionary strategies

I’d especially love feedback on:

* whether a similarity metric like this is useful
* how you’d validate it
* what failure modes you’d expect

If you’re curious to test it or contribute feedback, we’ve opened a small beta-testing group here:  
[**https://stratosphere-trading.com/**](https://stratosphere-trading.com/)

Happy to also share more technical details if there’s interest.
